behaviors.description: The speech-to-text output preserves speaker labels so that the summary can attribute statements to specific attendees.
behaviors.justification: Speaker-label preservation is fundamentally a diarization/model-quality behavior of the real speech-to-text model. A mock STT response can hide label-loss, label-swapping, or unstable speaker attribution that appears only with real audio and real model inference.
behaviors.test_description: Run the service on a real multi-speaker meeting recording with known speaker turns, using the real STT API. Assert the produced transcript includes speaker labels aligned to known speakers, and that downstream attribution fields can be populated from those labels without fallback heuristics.

behaviors.description: The structured summary contains at least one action item for every attendee who was assigned a task during the meeting.
behaviors.justification: This is extraction/completeness quality of LLM summarization, not just wiring. Mocking the summarizer would likely always satisfy schema and miss real omission failures where the model drops an assignee or task.
behaviors.test_description: Use real transcripts (or real STT outputs) containing explicitly assigned tasks per attendee, call the real LLM summarization step, and assert each attendee with an assigned task appears in action items at least once. Run repeatedly and fail on consistent under-coverage beyond an agreed threshold.

behaviors.description: Key decisions mentioned in the transcript appear in the "decisions" section of the summary, not buried in the narrative.
behaviors.justification: Correct section placement is a real LLM behavior (instruction following + semantic extraction). Mocks can enforce ideal JSON shape and miss cases where decisions are present only in free text or omitted from the dedicated field.
behaviors.test_description: Provide real transcripts with clearly marked decisions, invoke the real LLM summarizer, and assert those decisions are present in the structured "decisions" field (not only in narrative text). Include multiple runs to detect model variance in section placement.

behaviors.description: When the audio file is longer than 60 minutes, the service splits it into segments and processes them sequentially, merging the results into a single coherent summary.
behaviors.justification: The primary failure boundary is orchestration logic: segmentation thresholds, ordering, sequential processing, and merge behavior across modules. A pure unit test can miss cross-module state/order bugs; full real-model dependence is not strictly required to catch core failures.
behaviors.test_description: Using local fixtures that simulate a >60-minute timeline, run the segmentation-orchestration pipeline with deterministic stubbed STT/summary outputs per segment. Assert segment boundaries, strict sequential execution, complete coverage of all segments, and deterministic merged structured output with no dropped or duplicated segment content.

behaviors.description: The full pipeline — upload audio, transcribe, summarize, deliver structured output — completes without manual intervention for a real meeting recording.
behaviors.justification: This explicitly requires end-to-end execution across multiple real AI steps (STT then LLM summarization) plus delivery. Real failures often emerge only across step boundaries, retries, formats, and state handoffs in the full pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: Execute a production-like end-to-end run on a real meeting audio file: upload, real STT call, real LLM summary generation, and final structured output delivery. Assert successful completion status, valid structured schema (action items/decisions/attributions), and zero manual correction or rerun steps.
